decks.notes: This deck focuses on locking down opponents with a combination of powerful artifacts, control spells, and mana acceleration. By utilizing Urza's Saga to tutor up key pieces like Trinisphere, Planar Nexus, and The One Ring, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while generating value from cards like Ugin, Eye of the Storms and Karn, the Great Creator. The inclusion of powerful Eldrazi like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn adds a win condition that can quickly close out games. The deck's strategy is to establish a prison-like board state that is difficult for the opponent to overcome.

The main idea of this Boros Aggro Legacy deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures like Hexing Squelcher, Amped Raptor, and Ocelot Pride while using Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Swords to Plowshares for removal and protection. The deck also utilizes static prison to control the opponent's actions and Goblin Bombardment for additional damage. With a mix of efficient creatures, removal spells, and land destruction, the deck aims to quickly apply pressure and close out the game before the opponent can stabilize.

This Esper Vial deck focuses on using Aether Vial to cheat in powerful creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Overlord of the Balemurk. It also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will, Meddling Mage, and Lavinia, Azorius Renegade to control the board. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for extra value with the flicker effect. The deck aims to apply pressure early with aggro creatures while disrupting the opponent's game plan with counterspells and removal, ultimately overwhelming them with powerful creatures and Vial shenanigans.

This deck focuses on quickly playing powerful artifacts and using them to overwhelm the opponent. The strategy involves ramping up mana with Urza's lands and Ancient Tomb, while also using cards like Stock Up and Thundertrap Trainer to draw additional resources. Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, Eye of the Storms provide powerful control options, while The One Ring and Planar Nexus offer win conditions. Pithing Needle and Force of Will help to protect key cards, making it difficult for opponents to disrupt the deck's game plan.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through a combination of counter spells like Force of Will and Daze, alongside hand disruption like Thoughtseize. It also utilizes efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out to control the board. The creatures in the deck, like Murktide Regent and Orcish Bowmasters, help apply pressure on the opponent while also providing additional disruption. The deck aims to maintain tempo by controlling the game early on and steadily building its advantage to secure a win.

The main concept of this Dragon Stompy deck is to establish early board presence and disrupt opponents' mana bases with aggressive plays like Magus of the Moon and Trinisphere. The deck relies on fast mana acceleration from cards like Chrome Mox and City of Traitors to deploy threats such as Pyrogoyf and Broadside Bombardiers quickly. Chalice of the Void and Urza's Saga provide additional disruption and card advantage, while cards like Fury and Sundering Eruption offer removal and burn spells to close out the game. The goal is to overwhelm opponents with efficient threats and disrupt their game plan to secure a victory.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing lands to disrupt the opponent's strategy and establish a dominant board presence. It includes powerful land cards like Dark Depths, Wasteland, and Urza's Saga to control the battlefield and generate advantage. Crop Rotation and Life from the Loam are used to tutor for key lands and recur them from the graveyard. The deck also includes various utility lands like Maze of Ith and Ghost Quarter to handle different threats. Overall, the deck aims to grind out the opponent and win through land synergies and control.

The deck focuses on quickly setting up the combo of "Show and Tell" to put powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play without paying their mana cost. By using cards like Omniscience and Stock Up, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats while maintaining card advantage through card draw spells like Brainstorm and Ponder. The deck also includes protection in the form of counterspells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce, as well as a toolbox of cards like Cunning Wish to deal with different situations.

The main focus of this UWx Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan through counterspells, removal spells, and land destruction while drawing cards to maintain card advantage. Using powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo and Teferi, along with efficient removal like Swords to Plowshares, the deck aims to control the game tempo and win through incremental advantages. Additionally, the deck includes Daze, Stifle, and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's resources and slow down their game plan, allowing the control player to dictate the pace of the game.

The main concept of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan through hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize, while also utilizing aggressive creatures like Orcish Bowmasters and Murktide Regent to pressure the opponent's life total. The deck also focuses on tempo plays with cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Daze to control the pace of the game. The mana base is optimized to support both the aggressive and disruptive elements of the deck, with a mix of fetch lands, dual lands, and utility lands like Wasteland and Undercity Sewers.

This Legacy 1 vs 1 combo deck focuses on generating a high storm count to cast a powerful Tendrils of Agony to finish off the opponent. The deck includes fast mana sources like Mox Opal and Lion's Eye Diamond, along with rituals like Dark Ritual to generate mana quickly. The deck also includes cards like Silence to disrupt opponents, along with card draw like Brainstorm and Echo of Eons. Burning Wish allows the player to tutor for key combo pieces, while Song of Creation and Gaea's Will provide additional ways to generate mana and storm count.

The main concept of this Combo - Doomsday deck is to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to win the game by either casting Thassa's Oracle or using the Doomsday spell to set up a winning pile of cards in your library. The deck includes powerful tutors like Thoughtseize and Ponder to find the necessary pieces, along with disruptive cards like Force of Will and Veil of Summer to protect the combo. The deck also utilizes efficient mana generation with cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to quickly execute the combo and win the game.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and efficiently cheat out powerful game-ending creatures, such as Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Archon of Cruelty, using cards like Sneak Attack and Show and Tell. The deck also includes cards like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect the combo pieces, and utility lands like City of Traitors and Ancient Tomb to help ramp into the big plays. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its powerful creatures and win the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Aluren enchantment to create a combo with various creatures that allow for free casting or additional benefits when entering the battlefield. The archetype relies heavily on generating mana through cards like Gaea's Cradle and Noble Hierarch to quickly assemble the necessary pieces for the combo. By efficiently tutoring for key creatures using Green Sun's Zenith and Natural Order, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures like Craterhoof Behemoth while also maintaining control with cards like Endurance and Sylvan Safekeeper.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize artifacts and blue creatures to create a fast and aggressive gameplay style. By including cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Thoughtcast, the deck is able to quickly draw cards and play powerful artifact spells. The inclusion of cards like Urza's Saga and Mishra's Bauble allow for additional resources to be generated, while cards like Pinnacle Emissary and Patchwork Automaton provide strong creature threats. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of artifact synergy and aggressive creatures.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. It utilizes a combination of white and blue cards to maintain board control and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and versatile cards like Snapcaster Mage allows for flexibility in dealing with threats. Additionally, the deck features key cards like Swords to Plowshares and Terminus to handle creatures efficiently. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent and win through incremental advantage and disruption.
